turns.content: remember how I was hyped about doing shows in Jamaica?? yeah that's DEAD fam
Oh no, what happened? That sounded like it was going to be a really meaningful project for you. What changed?
I finally sat down and ran ALL the numbers and it's not even close to making sense. like I was living in a fantasy thinking I could pull this off right now
Running the numbers is the responsible thing to do though, even when the result hurts. What were the big costs that made it impossible?

flights, accommodation for however many nights, and the BIGGEST one — sound equipment rental down there. I can't bring my own gear on the plane obviously. and renting proper DJ equipment and PA systems in Jamaica is NOT cheap

Yeah, equipment rental in smaller markets can be surprisingly expensive because there's less competition. And flying gear is usually not practical or affordable either. What did the total picture look like?

I'd lose about $6,000. SIX THOUSAND DOLLARS. the venues down there just can't pay what I'd need to break even. like the money they're offering wouldn't even cover the equipment rental alone, forget the flights and hotel

Losing $6,000 would be a serious hit, especially as an independent artist. It's one thing to break even or take a small loss for exposure or personal reasons, but $6,000 in the hole is hard to justify at any stage of your career.
EXACTLY. and it's not like I'm Drake where I can just eat a $6,000 loss for the vibes!! that's like 3 months of studio rent. so yeah. forget about it. the Jamaica tour is NOT happening

That's a tough but smart decision. It doesn't mean it can never happen -- it just means right now isn't the time. There are ways it could work in the future:

- If you build a bigger name and can command higher fees from the venues
- If a sponsor or promoter covers the logistics
- If you connect with a Jamaican DJ or promoter who has equipment and can split costs

The dream doesn't have to die, just the timeline.

yeah maybe when I'm bigger and someone ELSE is paying for the flights lol. right now I gotta be smart with my money still. I was so excited about reconnecting with the roots and everything but $6,000 in the red?? nah. DEAD

You're being really mature about it. A lot of artists would have just gone ahead and dealt with the financial fallout after. The fact that you ran the numbers first shows good business sense.

And reconnecting with your roots doesn't have to be an expensive tour -- there might be smaller ways to stay connected musically with Jamaica without losing thousands of dollars.

true still. I could do online collabs with producers down there for way less. like send stems back and forth, build some connections that way. and then when the ting makes financial sense I can go do it properly with a real budget

That's a great alternative. Remote collaboration is how a lot of international music gets made now anyway. You could:

1. Connect with Jamaican producers and DJs online -- build the relationships first
2. Maybe do a single guest appearance at one venue when you visit family, rather than a full tour
3. Build your profile so that when you do go, promoters are coming to you with real budgets

The groundwork you lay now could make the actual tour way more successful when the time is right.

yeah that's the move. ok I'm at peace with it now. still stings a little but I'd rather be smart than broke ahlie!! can't make riddims if the lights are off in the lab lol. anyway thanks for letting me vent about it

Anytime! Venting is part of processing the decision. You made the right call -- protect the lab, keep building, and Jamaica will be there when the numbers work. In the meantime, those online collabs could open doors you haven't even thought of yet.
